commit.message:
Release: thread endpoint + briefing model + TS fix (#38)

* [claude-code] docs: update TASK-STATUS.md with all completed fixes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [claude-code] feat: add legacy bcrypt+JWT auth fallback for register and login

P0 fix — users were blocked from logging in when Supabase auth failed.
Now register and login both try Supabase first, then fall back to local
bcrypt password hashing + JWT token generation. Registration always
stores passwordHash for dual-mode support. Refresh endpoint handles
legacy JWT re-signing when no refresh token exists.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [claude-code] fix: seed-anil stores bcrypt passwordHash for legacy login fallback

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [claude-code] docs: QA pass results — 2026-04-03 TESTING lane

- Backend build: PASS (Prisma + tsc clean)
- Backend tests: 258/260 pass (2 auth.test.ts failures, not regression)
- Frontend build: FAIL (stale .next cache, ENOENT pages-manifest.json)
- Backend health: PASS (Railway prod ok)
- Frontend health: PASS (Vercel prod 200)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [claude-code] fix: add Zod validation to unvalidated POST/PATCH handlers

- drafts: createDraftSchema + updateDraftSchema replace raw req.body destructuring
- analytics: learningLogSchema replaces manual event check

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [claude-code] fix: replace console.warn/error with pino logger in auth, redis, supabase, pipeline, providers

Scripts (seed, smoke-test) intentionally keep console.log for CLI output.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [claude-code] fix: mount loopRouter at /api/loop — was defined but never registered

Fixes loop.state and loop.createPR returning 404. Found by Cluster 4 endpoint audit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [claude-code] docs: QA results — 2026-04-02 Run 2

Backend build PASS, tests 257/260 (flaky timeouts), production healthy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [claude-code] feat: add POST /drafts/:id/refine and POST /voice/calibrate routes

- drafts.refine: takes instruction, runs generation pipeline with it as
  feedback, updates draft in-place with refined content
- voice.calibrate: fetches tweets by handle via Twitter API, runs Claude
  calibration, upserts voice profile with calibrated dimensions

Both routes were wired in the frontend (api.ts) but had no backend
endpoints. Loop router was already mounted in a prior commit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: update auth tests for bcrypt fallback — register returns 200, login returns 401

The legacy bcrypt auth fallback (commit 046d2c8) changed behavior when
Supabase is null: register now succeeds via bcrypt, login returns 401
when user not found. Tests expected 503 from the old Supabase-only path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: alert ownership security, auth test dual-mode, Zod validation hardening

- Alert ownership: GET/PATCH/DELETE /:id now verify userId ownership (CODEX-01)
- Auth tests: updated for dual-mode Supabase + legacy JWT fallback, mock bcrypt
- Zod validation: added input schemas to drafts, images, research, voice routes
- New tests: alerts ownership, rateLimit middleware, voice, research, images routes
- 31 suites, 282 tests passing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: add S3 SDK and backup scripts for database backups

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add pagination, timeout utilities and R2 backup scripts

- lib/pagination.ts: cursor-based pagination helper for list endpoints
- lib/timeout.ts: promise timeout wrapper for external calls
- scripts/backup-to-r2.ts: database backup to Cloudflare R2
- scripts/BACKUP_SETUP.md: backup configuration guide

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Harden route error handling and add regression tests

* Add unit tests for alert delivery and rate limiting

* feat(analytics): sort days-to-peak results ascending + add test coverage (BO 54) (#24)

Sort peaks by days ascending per spec. Add 7 test cases for the
/api/analytics/days-to-peak endpoint: auth, role guard, happy path,
edge cases (no drafts, no analysts), sort order, handle fallback.

Co-authored-by: Alex Peri <aitkenassociates@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add Swagger UI and OpenAPI spec for Atlas API

* Reset draft route mocks and extend auth test timeout

* Stabilize auth and drafts unit tests

* fix: provider timeout, ownership checks, draft route cleanup (Codex + Claude)

Squash-merge lane/backend-phase8-fixes (PR #26):
- Set maxRetries=0 on AI providers, use pino logger in router
- Add ownership checks to alert CRUD endpoints (CODEX-01)
- Clean up draft route tests and reduce test duplication

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix: resolve all 12 test failures — analytics, drafts, images

- Analytics: use expectSuccessResponse() for days-to-peak response wrapper
- Drafts: fix error message assertions to match buildErrorResponse format
- Drafts: add ZodError handling to PATCH route (was returning 500 for validation)
- Images: fix error response assertions to match error() wrapper format

All 324 tests now passing across 35 suites.

- Login: 5 → 20 requests/min (users making typos were getting locked out)
- Register: 3 → 10 requests/min
- Add seed-alex.ts to upsert Alex's account with correct credentials

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: harden image generation — explicit API key check, safe JSON parse, better logging

- Throw clear error when GOOGLE_AI_API_KEY is missing instead of silent crash
- Wrap JSON.parse in try-catch with structured fallback for malformed Gemini responses
- Add stack traces to error logging in image routes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: simplify engagement-daily to return numeric actual (no null) + add test coverage

- engagement-daily endpoint now always returns `actual: 0` instead of `null` when no engagement data exists, preventing frontend rendering issues
- Add test for missing GOOGLE_AI_API_KEY guard in image generation
- Add loop route test suite

* fix: resolve TS2790 in loop.test.ts — delete on non-optional property

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: correct loop test assertion — route returns 503 not 500

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add session coordination bridge files

New .coordination/ directory with STATUS.json and CODEX-BRIEFING.md
for multi-session coordination. Auto-generated, never hand-edited.

New sessions read .coordination/STATUS.json before starting work.

commit.message:
Add comprehensive NDJSON raw logging layer for offline L0/L1/L2 analysis

Every NDJSON event now carries run_id and schema_version. The log is
bounded by run_started / run_finished (terminal marker) so an offline
job can detect incomplete runs. API calls are split into
api_call_started / api_call_finished with stable call_id, usage tokens,
finish_reason, and raw response data. Normalizers return _normMeta
describing which extraction path was taken (json, keyword fallback,
default, truncation). Utterance cleaning reports per-step booleans
(speaker prefix, actions, hallucination, min-length, dedup). Collision
resolution emits per-round collision_round events across all four tiers.
Interruption evaluation emits a single interruption_evaluation event
with all listeners, representative selection, and resolution method.

No changes to discussion semantics, prompt wording, or resolution logic.

commit.message:
将 SQL 与 zsh 切换到 crate-backed Tree-sitter parser 以降低冷构建耗时 (#4)

## 背景

这次主要目标不是继续微调 SQL grammar 本身，而是尽量降低 cold build 时 Tree-sitter parser
生成带来的关键路径耗时。

前面的构建 profile 已经确认，冷构建里最重的两项是 SQL 和 zsh 的 parser 生成。相比继续在 grammar
规则层面做个位数优化，这次改动改成直接使用上游 Rust grammar crate 提供的预生成 parser，避免在 `kat` 自己的
`build.rs` 里重复生成 `parser.c`。

## 变更

- 将 SQL parser 切换为 `tree-sitter-sequel` crate backend
- 将 zsh parser 切换为 `tree-sitter-zsh` crate backend
- 在 `grammars/registry.toml` 中增加 `parser_source = "crate"`，让 parser 来源成为
registry 级约定，而不是 `build.rs` 里的语言特判
- 让 `build.rs` 对 crate-backed grammar 跳过本地 `tree-sitter-generate` /
native parser build
- 删除仓库里已经失效的本地 SQL / zsh parser 源文件，仅保留本地 queries 和集成逻辑
- 更新第三方归属与架构/维护文档，明确 SQL / zsh 现在是 crate-backed grammar

## 验证

- `cargo build --release --locked`
- `just test`
- 使用全新 `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` 做 release build，确认 SQL / zsh 已不再依赖仓库内本地 parser
文件
- build profile 中确认：
  - `grammar=sql source=crate skip-local-build`
  - `grammar=zsh source=crate skip-local-build`

## 说明

- 这次没有移除现有 SQL 方言 runtime，也没有移除本地 SQL / zsh queries
- `sql_postgres` / `sql_mysql` / `sql_sqlite` 仍然保留，现有 semantic overlay
与宿主注入逻辑也继续沿用
- 这次调整改变的是“底层 parser 来源”，不是上层方言分发模型

---------

Summary:
- update the unreleased runtime entry to reference the actual
  refactor commit hash

- this repo records short commit hashes in release-note-worthy
  changelog entries

  refactor commit was created

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor(runtime): move supervision to the host app

Summary:
- replace the OTP application entrypoint with a public
  EtherCAT.Runtime singleton supervisor
- keep the EtherCAT API singleton-shaped and update docs, tests,
  Mix tooling, and hardware scripts to boot the runtime explicitly

Rationale:
- EtherCAT should embed under Ogol or another host supervision tree
  instead of owning its own application lifecycle
- keeping the master singleton avoids threading runtime selectors
  through the public API while still moving lifecycle ownership
  outside the library

Validation:
- mix compile
- mix test --no-start test/ethercat_test.exs
  test/ethercat/api_resilience_test.exs
  test/ethercat/subscribe_test.exs

commit.message:
feat(runtime): expand nested runtime highlighting and CI coverage (#1)

## Why
- 现有 YAML / GitHub Actions 场景已经需要超出“按扩展名选 grammar”的能力，继续堆特判会让后续维护越来越脆。
- `proto` 和 `textproto` 虽然同属 protobuf 生态，但分别承载 schema 和文本数据，长期看应拆成独立
runtime。
- 现有 nested runtime 语义增强原本分散在少数语言路径里，需要抽到统一 semantic overlay 层，才能继续扩
GitHub Actions、shell、regex、SQL 和 JSDoc 这类场景。
- 需要先在 CI 里验证多目标构建是否稳定可用，并积累更可读的构建耗时与缓存基线。

## What
- 重构 runtime 检测与分发模型，引入 `document kind`、YAML profile、host resolver
和统一语言别名归一化。
- 为 GitHub Actions workflow / `action.yml` 增加场景化高亮，支持 `run` 按
`shell`、`defaults.run.shell` 和 template shell 分发，并补齐 `${{ ... }}`
expression、`if`、`uses`、`permissions`、`runs-on`、`shell` 等 profile-aware
overlay。
- 新增 `proto` / `textproto` runtime，以及 `powershell` / `batch` grammar 与
overlay；GitHub Actions 和 Justfile 里的 `pwsh` / `cmd` 现在也会进入对应 runtime。
- 抽象并推广通用 semantic overlay，覆盖 shell / regex / SQL / JSDoc，并为 PowerShell
/ Batch 增加轻量语义增强。
- 新增 fixture / showcase / 回归测试，并同步更新语言覆盖、架构说明和第三方归属文档。
- 重构构建矩阵 CI：vendor 仓库内 `sccache` action、补充 build timing observability、缓存
tree-sitter build outputs，并收敛 pnpm / Node 安装流程。

## Testing
- `cargo fmt --all`
- `just test`

---------

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Data-first semantics rework (#7)

* Clean up pre-modules semantics and make named records consistent

**Major Changes:**
1. Split transparent aliases, shapes, and nominal named types across syntax, lowering, registration, and typechecking.
2. Remove legacy field-only/mixed trait internals in favor of typed constraints plus shared structural helpers.
3. Make named products behave consistently with records for constructor spread updates, structural spreads, and record-pattern rest matching.
4. Normalize codegen around named types, field-function resolution, and recursive record/Dyn projection so intersection-heavy programs compile cleanly.
5. Refresh docs, examples, fixtures, LSP, and tree-sitter artifacts to match the new semantics and parser surface.

**Testing:**
✅ dune runtest --root /Users/zlw/src/marmoset/marmoset lib/frontend/typecheck lib/backend/go --force
✅ ./test/integration.sh intersections
✅ make integration
✅ npm run generate
✅ npm test

**Changes:**
- lib/frontend/syntax/*: parser/lowering/type updates for shapes, named types, and named-product constructor spread.
- lib/frontend/typecheck/*: typed constraint model, structural helper module, named type registry, subtype-aware intersection handling, and named-record pattern/spread support.
- lib/backend/go/emitter.ml: named type emission plus recursive projection for named/Dyn record coercions.
- docs/*, examples/*, test/fixtures/*, tools/*: documentation, fixtures, LSP, and tree-sitter updates aligned with the new language model.

* Reject effectful method calls in pure functions

**Problem:**
Pure (`->`) functions still allowed effectful method calls because purity checking only looked at `AST.Call` callee types and ignored resolved method effects on `AST.MethodCall`.

**Approach:**
- record effectfulness at method-call resolution time for real method dispatch, qualified inherent dispatch, and field-function fallback
- consult that call-site artifact during purity walking
- add unit and integration regressions for pure functions calling effectful methods

**Testing:**
- dune runtest --root /Users/zlw/src/marmoset/marmoset lib/frontend/typecheck --force
- ./test/integration.sh purity

**Changes:**
- lib/frontend/typecheck/infer.ml: track effectful method call sites and enforce them in purity checks
- test/fixtures/purity/p26_pure_fn_effectful_method_call_rejected.mr: regression fixture

* Reject alias-owned trait and inherent behavior

**Problem:**
Transparent `alias` targets were still allowed to own behavior. Trait impls expanded alias heads as if they were real owners, inherent impl target inference still treated aliases as known heads, and invalid alias impls could leak into predeclared impl headers.

**Approach:**
- reject alias heads before trait impl, inherent impl, and derive target conversion
- stop treating aliases as inherent-impl owner heads when collecting generic binders
- skip impl-header predeclaration for alias-headed trait impl targets so invalid impls cannot influence earlier resolution
- add checker and integration regressions for alias trait/inherent impl targets

**Testing:**
- dune runtest --root /Users/zlw/src/marmoset/marmoset lib/frontend/typecheck --force
- ./test/integration.sh traits_impl traits_inherent

**Changes:**
- lib/frontend/typecheck/infer.ml: validate alias-headed behavior targets and guard impl-header predeclaration
- lib/frontend/typecheck/checker.ml: add alias-behavior regression tests
- test/fixtures/traits_impl/ti38_trait_impl_target_transparent_alias_is_rejected.mr: negative integration case
- test/fixtures/traits_inherent/th49_inherent_impl_target_transparent_alias_is_rejected.mr: negative integration case

* Support field-path union narrowing

**Detailed explanation:**
- adds a shared Type_narrowing helper so the checker and Go emitter understand narrowing paths beyond bare identifiers
- extends type narrowing from local names to direct field projections such as box.value in both true and false branches
- preserves the simpler identifier-based Go output while emitting runtime subject temps only for projected paths

**Testing:**
- dune runtest --root /Users/zlw/src/marmoset/marmoset lib/frontend/typecheck lib/backend/go --force
- ./test/integration.sh unions
- adds direct projection narrowing coverage in checker, emitter, and integration fixtures

**Changes:**
- lib/frontend/typecheck/type_narrowing.ml: centralizes path extraction, substitution, and narrowing/complement logic
- lib/frontend/typecheck/infer.ml: rewrites if-branch narrowing to support field paths
- lib/backend/go/emitter.ml: lowers projected-path narrowing through runtime type switches
- lib/frontend/typecheck/checker.ml: adds regression coverage
- test/fixtures/unions/u90_field_path_narrowing_on_record_projection.mr: verifies end-to-end behavior

* Support union match narrowing for record patterns

**Detailed explanation:**
- teaches record patterns to narrow union-typed match scrutinees member-by-member instead of checking every arm against the whole union
- narrows the matched scrutinee inside arm bodies, so code like `case { x: }: v.x` typechecks against the concrete matched member
- adds a dedicated union-match codegen path that switches on the concrete runtime member, then reuses record-pattern arm lowering for the filtered matching arms

**Testing:**
- dune runtest --root /Users/zlw/src/marmoset/marmoset lib/frontend/typecheck --force
- dune runtest --root /Users/zlw/src/marmoset/marmoset lib/backend/go --force
- ./test/integration.sh unions

**Changes:**
- lib/frontend/typecheck/infer.ml: tracks narrowed match-arm member types and applies them to bindings and scrutinee-path rewrites
- lib/frontend/typecheck/checker.ml: adds a regression for `match` narrowing on named-product unions
- lib/backend/go/emitter.ml: lowers union record-pattern matches through concrete-member switches and shared record-arm emission
- test/fixtures/unions/u91_match_record_patterns_narrow_union_named_products.mr: verifies end-to-end execution

* Update docs

* Commit plan

* Align feature docs with data-first semantics target

**Detailed explanation:**
- updates the rework plan's PROGRESS section with current findings, caveats, and verification
- adds short status notes to the relevant feature docs so the repo documents the target data-first model before parser/checker changes land
- shifts those notes toward transparent `type` names, constructor-based nominality, and match-first wrapper destructuring without rewriting the full public docs yet

**Testing:**
- `make unit compiler`

**Changes:**
- docs/plans/todo/language/data-first-semantics-rework.md: add live progress tracking
- docs/features/records.md: note structural-record target and wrapper projection guidance
- docs/features/type-annotations-and-aliases.md: note `type`-first transparent naming target
- docs/features/inherent-methods.md: note exact-type UFCS extension model
- docs/features/pattern-matching.md: note constructor-pattern-first wrapper destructuring

* Make transparent type structural in compiler

**Detailed explanation:**
This pass reinterprets plain \Name not found
= not found
... not found declarations as transparent aliases throughout parsing, lowering, type checking, and code generation while preserving explicit constructor-bearing wrappers as nominal. It also aligns match-pattern sugar and backend expectations with the data-first semantics plan and records the completed checkpoint in the plan progress log.

**Testing:**
- dune runtest --root /Users/zlw/src/marmoset/marmoset lib/frontend/syntax/ --force
- dune runtest --root /Users/zlw/src/marmoset/marmoset lib/frontend/typecheck/ --force
- dune runtest --root /Users/zlw/src/marmoset/marmoset lib/backend/go/ --force
- make unit compiler

**Changes:**
- lower transparent \ declarations to \, keep explicit \ wrappers nominal, and add unqualified/flattened constructor-pattern parsing
- resolve trait impls, inherent impls, and derives for transparent \ targets by exact underlying type instead of rejecting them as non-owning aliases
- update checker and Go emitter tests to use structural record values for transparent types, record the backend registry-isolation caveat, and keep the plan PROGRESS section current

* Update tooling for transparent type syntax

**Detailed explanation:**
Adjust tree-sitter and LSP tooling to treat plain type declarations as the primary transparent surface, while still understanding explicit wrapper and constructor-bearing type forms. This updates the grammar, corpus coverage, generated parser artifacts, symbol classification, and keyword completion behavior so editor integrations track the new data-first surface.

**Testing:**
- npm test (in tools/tree-sitter-marmoset)
- dune runtest --root /Users/zlw/src/marmoset/marmoset tools/lsp/lib/ --force

**Changes:**
- update tree-sitter grammar, queries, corpora, and generated parser outputs for transparent type and wrapper forms
- classify transparent type declarations as struct-like symbols in the LSP
- remove legacy alias keyword completion and refresh completion descriptions for type declarations

* Align compiler and fixtures with transparent type semantics

**Detailed explanation:**
Finish the data-first transparent type migration across the compiler and test surface. Derive expansion now predeclares transparent type aliases before checking user-trait superconstraints, which restores derives over structural record types. The remaining fixtures and active docs/examples are updated from nominal constructor-style transparent types to the structural or explicit-wrapper forms required by the plan, and the plan progress log now reflects the completed validation.

**Testing:**
- dune runtest --root /Users/zlw/src/marmoset/marmoset lib/frontend/typecheck/ --force
- make integration function_model/fm063_error_wrong_receiver_qualified_call.mr traits/t117_user_trait_derive_same_clause_satisfies_supertrait.mr traits_inherent/th01_inherent_method_on_type_alias_int.mr traits_impl/ti38_trait_impl_target_transparent_alias_is_rejected.mr traits_inherent/th49_inherent_impl_target_transparent_alias_is_rejected.mr vnext_canary/vn104_user_trait_derive_structural_field_supertrait_missing.mr vnext_canary/vn109_user_trait_derive_sees_late_supertrait_impl.mr vnext_canary/vn113_user_trait_derive_dyn_child_exposes_supertrait_method.mr vnext_canary/vn90_user_trait_derive_supertrait_already_impl_success.mr
- make unit compiler
- make integration

**Changes:**
- predeclare transparent type aliases during derive expansion so shape-backed user derives resolve correctly before the main inference predeclaration pass
- rewrite the remaining structural-record fixtures from constructor-style transparent types to structural literals, explicit wrappers, or updated positive expectations where the plan changed behavior
- update active docs, examples, and the plan progress section to describe plain type as the transparent surface and wrappers as the nominal form

* Remove alias surface and canonicalize sum-type syntax

**Detailed explanation:**
- Removes the legacy `alias` keyword from the frontend and tooling surface.
- Canonicalizes constructor-bearing `type Name = { Variant(...) }` as the primary sum-type form while keeping `enum` as compatibility sugar through the same surface AST path.
- Migrates the remaining executable fixtures, tests, docs, and diagnostics to transparent `type` syntax so the branch tells one coherent data-first story again.

**Testing:**
- `dune runtest --root /Users/zlw/src/marmoset/marmoset lib/frontend/syntax/ --force`
- `npm run generate` (in `tools/tree-sitter-marmoset`)
- `npm test` (in `tools/tree-sitter-marmoset`)
- `dune runtest --root /Users/zlw/src/marmoset/marmoset tools/lsp/lib/ --force`
- `dune runtest --root /Users/zlw/src/marmoset/marmoset lib/frontend/typecheck/ --force`
- `make unit compiler`
- `make integration`

**Changes:**
- `lib/frontend/syntax/*`: remove `alias` tokens/parsing, unify `enum` sugar with `type` sum parsing, and lower sum-style `type` through a canonical `STNamedSum` path.
- `lib/frontend/typecheck/*` and `lib/backend/go/emitter.ml`: update diagnostics/tests and downstream expectations for transparent `type` syntax.
- `tools/tree-sitter-marmoset/*`, `tools/lsp/lib/doc_symbols.ml`, and editor grammars: remove `alias` surface support and align tooling with canonical `type` syntax.
- `test/fixtures/*`, docs, and the plan log: migrate executable coverage and record the completed follow-up work.

* Align editor plugins with shape and canonical type syntax

**Detailed explanation:**
- Fix stale Zed and Neovim tree-sitter queries that still referenced removed nodes like type_alias and trait_field.
- Add shape-aware highlighting, outline, folding, indentation, and textobject support across the editor plugins.
- Extend the VS Code and JetBrains TextMate grammars so shape definitions, canonical constructor-bodied type declarations, and wrapper types are highlighted explicitly.
- Tighten editor regression checks so future syntax shifts fail CI instead of silently leaving plugin support behind.

commit.message:
fix(automations): batch log appends to eliminate OCC errors (#94)

* fix(automations): batch log appends to eliminate OCC errors on automation_run_logs

Collapse N individual appendAutomationRunLog mutations per callback
request into a single appendAutomationRunLogBatch transaction. This
eliminates the hot-spot serialization point where every concurrent log
append read and wrote the same automation_runs document, causing
OptimisticConcurrencyControlFailure on retry exhaustion.

Key changes:
- Add appendAutomationRunLogBatchInternal that inserts all lines and
  patches automation_runs metadata once per batch instead of per line
- Refactor single-line appendAutomationRunLogInternal to delegate to
  the batch function
- Replace unbounded .collect() in eviction path with bounded .take()
- Update log callback handler to chunk lines into batches of 50

Closes #70
